author: @gotoluc
text:
Hi Robert,

the reason you did not see any improvements by adding the outside cage is that the south pole flux of the magnets are lost in those thick transformer lamination cores and not reaching your steel pipe.

Luc

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I've tried to follow your link but can't seem to find it - can you repost?

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Hey valve man, it's not quite a standard solenoid is it? If you do a patent search you'll find a couple using this principle and although I agree it is solenoid at heart it is more efficient than other designs. Personally I stopped looking at it because I think it is bait of a free energy dead end. But it is interesting and you learn as much from things that don't work as things that do. To my mind constant experimentation is the way forward as is shared CONSTRUCTIVE criticism.

author: @valveman12
text: OK so you built a solenoid.  So where is the power amplification and free energy?  If you search youtube, you will see lots of videos of people who have antique solenoid operating flywheels.  Some are even new.  Converting electrical energy to mechanical is certainly not new and it is ceratinly not free energy.

author: @gigaleeboy
text: Thanks for the update!  I can't wait to see it!

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Hi, I built a flywheel from lead and attached it and ran it - it went so fast it flew apart because i had just glued the magnets to the pushrod. So now i have to rebuild it but more strongly so that is what i am currently doing. My new construction is all steel and bolted and i have run a few finite analysis models to test out some of the design principles - unfortunately it is pretty time consuming - but i will put up a video when i have it working properly - cheers

author: @gigaleeboy
text: Nice.   May I ask what happened to the piston/fly wheel design?   It looked like you might have something
